vocab.meaning: người hướng ngoại, thích giao lưu với nhiều người
vocab.example: I'm not exactly a social butterfly, but I do enjoy a quick chat with a stranger when the mood strikes me.

vocab.word: get on like a house on fire
vocab.ipa: /ɡet ɒn laɪk ə haʊs ɒn ˈfaɪə(r)/
vocab.meaning: nhanh chóng trở nên thân thiết (với ai đó)
vocab.example: I once met a fellow traveler at the airport, and we got on like a house on fire during the three-hour delay.

vocab.meaning: một điều gì đó mới mẻ, dễ chịu (nói về tính cách hoặc cuộc trò chuyện)
vocab.example: Talking to that street artist was a breath of fresh air; his perspective on life was so different from my usual social circle.

vocab.meaning: tâm đầu ý hợp ngay lập tức
vocab.example: We sat next to each other on a cross-country bus and hit it off instantly, talking about our favorite books for hours.
It's surprising how quickly you can find common ground with a complete stranger if you just start with a simple question.

vocab.meaning: trở nên dạn dĩ, cởi mở hơn
vocab.example: The friendly old man at the park really helped me come out of my shell and engage in a wonderful conversation.

vocab.word: at the drop of a hat
vocab.meaning: ngay lập tức, không do dự
vocab.example: In my hometown, people are so friendly that they will start a long conversation with you at the drop of a hat.

vocab.meaning: làm một việc mạo hiểm (ở đây là chủ động bắt chuyện dù có thể bị từ chối)
vocab.example: I decided to go out on a limb and compliment the woman's unique coat, which led to a fascinating chat about fashion.

vocab.meaning: khả năng ăn nói lưu loát, thuyết phục
vocab.example: My brother has the gift of the gab; he can strike up a meaningful conversation with anyone, anywhere.

vocab.meaning: thích sự riêng tư, không hay giao du với người lạ
vocab.example: Generally, I tend to keep myself to myself in public, but occasionally a kind smile from someone will encourage me to speak up.
